title: memstead-plugin/v0 medium
description: A Medium is a passive, named, typed reference to a body of information the mem acknowledges as part of its territory — nothing more (no selection, no engagement metadata, no preparation). Each medium file lives at `.memstead/mediums/<mem>/<name>.json`; the filename stem is the medium's reference key, used by a facet's `medium` field. The four-primitive successor to the old `scope` file's territory half (Medium = territory; Facet = engagement).

name.description: Stable reference name — facets reference a medium by this. Conventionally equal to the filename stem.

type.description: What kind of surface this medium references. `codebase` = a source tree; `filesystem` = a non-code file tree; `graph` = another mem's graph (the reserved id `graph` is "home"); `git` = a commit history; `web` = web sources. `pdf` and other non-text mediums join with their follow-up plans.

pointer.description: Where the body of information lives — a path, URL, or mem id, interpreted per `type`. Opaque to the plugin. Workspace-relative paths may carry leading `../` to reach sibling code trees. May be empty when the territory base is the workspace root.

change_detection.description: How the ingest loop detects what changed in this medium since the last synced pass, so a fresh iteration steers at the changed slice instead of re-roaming. `auto` (default when omitted) probes for a git work tree over `pointer` — present → behave as `git`, absent → `mtime`. `git` = files differing between the stored commit id and the source's current HEAD. `mtime` = a per-file `{mtime,size}` stat-map diff (added/modified/deleted). `none` = no reliable signal; the cursor is inert and the ingest runs as today (use for `web` or any opted-out source). `graph`-type mediums ignore this field — their signal is the source mem's snapshot token, which the engine already provides.
